title: Racosperma gardneri
taxonomy: Racosperma gardneri is a species that was previously classified as Acacia gardneri. However, in 2006, it was reclassified under the genus Racosperma as part of a larger reclassification of the Acacia genus. This reclassification was an effort to better categorize and understand the relationships between different species within the genus. Racosperma gardneri is native to Western Australia, and its classification change reflects a shift in our understanding of the plant's evolutionary history. The new classification is still widely recognized in botanical circles today.

intro: Racosperma gardneri, also known as Acacia dealbata, is a plant species native to Australia, belonging to the Acacia genus of flowering shrubs and trees.
